pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh script (line 18) which creates LCA_Results directory and cleans this path

Task description explicitly lists these three groups as required. Grouping feature is confirmed to exist in OpenLCA per manual documentation at https://greendelta.github.io/openLCA2-manual/res_analysis/res_analysis_groups.html
USLCI database contains regional electricity processes. NREL and Federal LCA Commons documentation confirms presence of 'Electricity, at Grid' processes with Eastern Interconnection data available

pi_items.verified_value: Fossil (56.7% of generation, combining coal 18.4% and natural gas 38.3% for PJM/Eastern grid as of 2024)
pi_items.source: PJM (Pennsylvania-New Jersey-Maryland) Interconnection fuel mix data from 2024: gas-fired 38.3%, coal 18.4%, nuclear 33.6%. PJM is the largest RTO in Eastern Interconnection covering Mid-Atlantic and Midwest. https://www.monitoringanalytics.com/reports/PJM_State_of_the_Market/2024/2024q2-som-pjm-sec12.pdf

pi_items.verified_value: U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database maintained by NREL, distributed through Federal LCA Commons. Real, authoritative LCA database for US materials, products, and processes
pi_items.source: https://www.nlr.gov/analysis/lci and https://www.lcacommons.gov/. USLCI is official U.S. EPA and NREL resource

pi_items.verified_value: TRACI 2.1 (Tool for Reduction and Assessment of Chemicals and other environmental Impacts) is official EPA life cycle impact assessment methodology, available for OpenLCA in JSON zip format

pi_items.metadata_value: TRACI 2.1 (Global Warming Potential implied from task description)
pi_items.verified_value: Global Warming Potential (GWP) is one of TRACI 2.1's impact categories, appropriate for electricity grid analysis
pi_items.source: TRACI 2.1 documentation confirms GWP as major midpoint impact category. https://docs.earthster.org/en/articles/8177457-traci-2-1-impact-categories

pi_items.metadata_value: Eastern US electricity grid for process name matching
pi_items.verified_value: Eastern Interconnection serves eastern North America from Canada to Florida to Great Plains (excluding most of Texas). Includes major RTOs: PJM, ISO-NE (New England), NYISO, MISO. USLCI database contains regional electricity processes for this region
pi_items.source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eastern_Interconnection and Federal LCA Commons USLCI documentation

privileged_info_summary: The grid_mix_source_analysis task is based on real, authoritative data sources. The USLCI database is the official U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database maintained by NREL and available through the Federal LCA Commons. TRACI 2.1 is the EPA's established life cycle impact assessment methodology. The Eastern US electricity grid is a real interconnection serving the eastern United States with verified 2024 fuel composition: fossil fuels (coal 18.4% + natural gas 38.3% = 56.7%) constitute the dominant source for electricity generation in the PJM region (the largest RTO in the Eastern Interconnection), followed by nuclear (33.6%). The task requires grouping results into Fossil, Nuclear, and Renewable categories using OpenLCA's documented Grouping feature for result analysis. All output paths and process keywords are appropriate and align with actual USLCI database contents. Global Warming Potential is a standard impact category in TRACI 2.1 suitable for electricity generation assessment.
